reason: The remediation modifies /etc/fstab to add the nodev mount option on non‑root local partitions. Changing mount options can prevent partitions from mounting correctly, potentially causing boot failures or service disruptions. According to policy, filesystem/partition changes are classified as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; boot failure if fstab syntax is incorrect; service disruption if critical partitions fail to mount; potential loss of console/SSH access during reboot
